subtitle: How Vijayalaya Raised the Tiger Banner over Thanjavur

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of fortress-city and warrior
panels.action: Vijayalaya Chola stands on a rocky rise overlooking the distant fortified town of Thanjavur, one hand resting on his unsheathed sword, the other on a rolled tiger-banner, as monsoon clouds brood over the Kaveri plain.

dialogue.text: In the ninth century Kaveri land, Vijayalaya Chola rose from forgotten embers to seize Thanjavur and light the long-silent tiger of the Cholas.

i18n.hi: नवीं सदी की कावेरी भूमि में, भूली चिंगारियों से विजयालय चोल उठे, थंजावूर पर कब्ज़ा किया और चोलों के सोए शेर को जगाया।

panels.action: A broad view of the Kaveri delta shows green fields, winding canals, and small temples under the fading light, with distant banners of the Pallavas and the Muttaraiyars marking scattered forts.

dialogue.text: For centuries the Kaveri delta had known many masters. Pallava lords in Kanchipuram, Muttaraiyar chiefs in Thanjavur, fading Cholas remembered only in stone and song.

i18n.hi: सदियों तक कावेरी डेल्टा ने कई मालिक देखे। कांचीपुरम के पल्लव स्वामी, थंजावूर के मुत्तरैयार सरदार, और बस गीतों‑पत्थरों में बचे फीके चोल।

panels.action: Inside a modest but orderly Pallava-aligned chieftain’s hall, Vijayalaya Chola stands among other minor chiefs, his bearing calm but intent, as a Pallava officer issues commands before a map of the south.

dialogue.text: Vijayalaya first appears in stone as a chieftain under Pallava shadow, a lesser lord among many, offering spears and tribute while watching the great powers tire themselves in war.

i18n.hi: शिलालेखों में विजयालय पहले पल्लव छाया के अधीन एक सरदार बनकर दिखते हैं—बहुतों में एक छोटा नायक, जो भाले और कर चढ़ाता, और बड़े राजाओं को युद्ध में थकते देखता।

panels.action: Close on Vijayalaya Chola’s weathered face, eyes narrowed, lit by the flicker of an oil lamp as he studies an old inscription carved on a stone slab bearing the Chola tiger emblem.

dialogue.text: He knew the tiger crest was old. Copper plates and crumbling walls still whispered of ancient Cholas, once kings of this very Kaveri land.

i18n.hi: वह जानता था शेर का चिह्न पुराना है। ताम्रपत्र और जर्जर दीवारें अब भी उन प्राचीन चोलों की फुसफुसाहट थी, जो कभी इसी कावेरी भूमि के राजा थे।

panels.shot: medium shot around a campaign map
panels.action: Vijayalaya leans over a rough palm-leaf map spread on a low table, tracing routes toward Thanjavur with his finger while a few trusted retainers watch in tense silence.

dialogue.text: Yet the tiger line had broken. The Pallavas held the north, the Pandyas watched from the south, and in between, Muttaraiyar chiefs ruled from Thanjavur, collecting the Kaveri’s wealth.

i18n.hi: पर शेर की वंशरेखा टूट चुकी थी। उत्तर में पल्लव, दक्षिण से ताकते पांड्य, और बीच में थंजावूर से राज करते मुत्तरैयार, जो कावेरी की दौलत समेटते थे।

panels.shot: wide shot of battlefield between great powers
panels.action: A distant battlefield shows Pallava and Pandya forces clashing on a dusty plain, elephants and horsemen colliding, while a smaller, separate Chola contingent watches from a low hill, not yet committed.

dialogue.text: In these years the Pallava and Pandya lions locked horns again and again. Their wars shook the south, but also left gaps—unguarded passes, weary garrisons, restless vassals.

i18n.hi: इन वर्षों में पल्लव और पांड्य सिंह बार‑बार भिड़े। उनके युद्धों ने दक्खिन हिला दिया, पर साथ ही खाली जगहें छोड़ीं—अनसुरक्षित दर्रे, थकी चौकियाँ, बैचैन सामंत।

panels.shot: medium shot in war camp at night
panels.action: In a dim Pallava war camp, Vijayalaya sits apart on a wooden stool, removing his helmet, listening as a scout kneels before him, gesturing toward the direction of Thanjavur.

dialogue.text: From campfires and scouts’ whispers, Vijayalaya heard news that mattered more than any single battle. Thanjavur’s Muttaraiyar lord was pressed, its defences stretched thin.

i18n.hi: मचानों की आग और जासूसों की सरगोशियों से विजयालय ने वह खबर सुनी जो किसी एक युद्ध से बड़ी थी—थंजावूर का मुत्तरैयार स्वामी घिर चुका है, उसकी रक्षा ढीली है।

panels.action: Vijayalaya rises, jaw set, as if coming to a decision; behind him, a faint carving of the Chola tiger on his shield catches the lamplight.

dialogue.text: The copper plates of Tiruvalangadu would later say, ‘Vijayalaya took Tanjai from the Muttaraiyan’. In this moment, that line is only a thought: seize the city, awaken the house.

i18n.hi: तिरुवालंगाडु के ताम्रपत्र बाद में लिखेंगे, ‘विजयालय ने मुट्टारैयन से तंजई छीनी’। इस घड़ी वह बस एक ख्याल था—शहर पर कब्ज़ा करो, घराने को जगाओ।

panels.action: A lean but disciplined Chola-led force marches along a countryside path under dawn light: spearmen, archers, and a few elephants moving in silence behind Vijayalaya on horseback.

dialogue.text: He did not yet command a great army. He had men loyal to his house, some bought with coin, some bound by clan, enough to move swiftly where empires were slow.

i18n.hi: उसके पास तब कोई विराट सेना न थी। कुछ लोग जो वंश के प्रति वफादार थे, कुछ सिक्कों से खरीदे, कुछ कुल से बंधे—बस इतना कि वह वहाँ पहुँच सके जहाँ साम्राज्य सुस्त थे।

panels.shot: wide shot of Thanjavur fort exterior
panels.action: The fortifications of Thanjavur rise over the plain, earthen ramparts and wooden palisades framing a busy gate, as Vijayalaya’s troops wait hidden among groves and irrigation bunds.

dialogue.text: Thanjavur was no mere town. It guarded canals and granaries, temples and treasuries. Whoever held it could feed armies and claim the heart of the Kaveri delta.

i18n.hi: थंजावूर कोई साधारण कस्बा न था। वह नहरों और कोठारों, मंदिरों और ख़ज़ानों की रखवाली करता। जो इसे पकड़े, वह सेना पाल सके और कावेरी डेल्टा का दिल अपना कहे।

panels.shot: medium shot of tense pre-assault moment
panels.action: In a shaded grove near the city walls, Vijayalaya grips his spear and crouches beside his captains, all listening to the distant sounds from the fort as if waiting for a signal in the wind.

dialogue.text: Later poems would dress the capture in bright legend. The truth the records keep is plainer: in a time of confusion, Vijayalaya struck, and the Muttaraiyar hold broke.

i18n.hi: बाद की कविताएँ इस विजय को चमकदार कथा में लपेटेंगी। पर अभिलेखों की सादी बात यही है—अफ़रा‑तफ़री के समय में विजयालय ने वार किया, और मुत्तरैयारों की पकड़ टूटी।

panels.action: Vijayalaya leads his soldiers in a determined advance toward the open gate of Thanjavur fort, shields raised, as defenders on the rampart stand scattered and unprepared.

dialogue.speaker: vijayalaya_chola
dialogue.text: Forward. Take the city, and take our name back with it.

i18n.hi: आगे बढ़ो। शहर लो, और साथ में हमारा नाम भी वापस।

panels.action: Inside the fort courtyard, the scene is one of tense, smoky aftermath: Muttaraiyar standards lie cast down, armed men of Vijayalaya stand watchful, and townsfolk peer from doorways at their new masters.

dialogue.text: There is no inscription for the cries, the flight, the bargains made at sword’s edge. Only this: when the dust settled, Thanjavur answered to Vijayalaya Chola.

i18n.hi: उन चीखों, भगदड़ों, और तलवार की नोक पर हुए सौदों का कोई शिलालेख नहीं। बस इतना दर्ज रहा—धूल बैठने पर थंजावूर, विजयालय चोल के हुक्म में था।

panels.action: On the inner rampart, Vijayalaya plants a tall banner-pole; at its top unfurls the Chola tiger emblem, rippling in the wind above the humbled fort and the watching soldiers.

dialogue.text: On that rampart rose the tiger once more. Not yet the roaring empire of later days, but a sign: a sleeper-house had claimed a city and dared to rule.

i18n.hi: उसी प्राचीर पर शेर फिर से उठा। अभी भावी साम्राज्य की दहाड़ नहीं, पर संकेत ज़रूर—सोया हुआ घराना उठकर एक शहर पर राज करने को तैयार हुआ।

panels.shot: medium shot at temple construction site
panels.action: Vijayalaya stands before the partly built structure of a stone temple, speaking with artisans and priests; in the background, masons haul carved blocks, and a small shrine to Durga rises.

dialogue.text: To mark his rule he raised temples in Thanjavur’s soil, inscriptions naming him as king, not mere chief. Stone, more than steel, would keep his memory.

i18n.hi: अपनी सत्ता का चिह्न बनाने उसने थंजावूर की मिट्टी में मंदिर खड़े किए, अभिलेखों में अपने को राजा कहलाया, केवल सरदार नहीं। उसकी स्मृति स्टील से ज़्यादा पत्थर ने बचाई।

panels.action: Vijayalaya pours libations in a temple sanctum, priests chanting beside him, as he bows before a goddess image, his sword laid respectfully on the stone floor.

dialogue.text: He honoured the gods and upheld old forms, for in this age kingship was as much ritual as war. Copper plates would call him ‘who conquered Tanjai and made it his shining seat’.

i18n.hi: उसने देवताओं को मान दिया और पुरानी रीतियाँ निभाईं, क्योंकि इस युग में राज होना जितना युद्ध था, उतना ही अनुष्ठान। ताम्रपत्र उसे कहेंगे, ‘जिसने तंजई जीती और इसे अपनी दीप्तिमान राजधानी बनाया’।

panels.shot: wide shot of Thanjavur under new rule
panels.action: A panoramic view of Thanjavur under Vijayalaya’s rule: bustling markets, new fortifications, canals under repair, and the Chola banner flying from several towers as the sun rises over the city.

dialogue.text: With Thanjavur secure, the Chola house gained more than walls. It gained land, peasants, revenue, a base from which his son and grandson would push out along the Kaveri’s veins.

i18n.hi: थंजावूर की सुरक्षा से चोल घराने को दीवारों से बढ़कर मिला—ज़मीन, किसान, आमदनी, और वह आधार, जिससे उसका बेटा‑पोता कावेरी की नसों के साथ‑साथ आगे बढ़े।

panels.action: A layered composition shows Vijayalaya in the foreground, older and seated on a simple throne, while in the mist behind him loom faint images of later Chola marvels: a great stone temple, ships on the sea, and distant crowned successors.

dialogue.text: From this single capture would grow the line of Aditya, Parantaka, Rajaraja, Rajendra. But in Vijayalaya’s own days, it was still a fragile beginning, held by grit and grain.

i18n.hi: इसी एक जीत से आदित्य, परांतक, राजराज, राजेंद्र की पुश्त बढ़ी। पर विजयालय के दिनों में यह बस नाज़ुक शुरुआत थी, जो हिम्मत और अनाज के दम पर टिकी थी।

panels.action: An engraved stone inscription fills the frame, its Tamil letters sharply carved, while the faint reflection of Vijayalaya’s figure appears on its polished surface as if he stands beyond time.

dialogue.text: In the records he is the first clear flame of the imperial Cholas. By seizing Thanjavur at the right hour, Vijayalaya turned a forgotten name into the seed of an ocean-spanning throne.

i18n.hi: अभिलेखों में वही साम्राज्यवादी चोलों की पहली साफ लौ हैं। सही घड़ी में थंजावूर पर कब्ज़ा कर विजयालय ने भूले हुए नाम को सागर छूती गद्दी का बीज बना दिया।

subtitle_i18n.en: How Vijayalaya Raised the Tiger Banner over Thanjavur
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे विजयालय ने थंजावूर पर शेर का ध्वज गाड़ा
